UT _mea/anfizfiuzmaatam-o— Womazafimaflrm, wizwmfimmfiml mmfil _ mmmawamm i ammmwmmafi I ml NATIONAL HIGHWAYS AUTHORITY or INDIA NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 21“ August, 2013 ‘The National Highways Authority of India (Recruitment, Seniority and Promotion) Fourth Amendment , Regulations, 2013 No. NHAI/11012/158/2010-HR. I (Vol. II). — In exercise of the powers conferred by section 35, read with section 9. of the National Highways Authority of India Act, 1988 (68 of 1988), the National Highways Authority of India hereby - makes the following regulations further to amend the National Highways Authority of India (Recruitment, Seniority and Promotion) Regulations, 1996, namely :— l. (1) These regulations may be called the National Highways Authority of India (Recruitment, Seniority and Promotion) Fourth Amendment Regulations, 2013. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Schedule to the National Highways Authority of India (Recruitment, Seniority and Promotion) Regulations, 1996 (hereinafier referred to as the principal regulations), the Schedule prescribing eligibility criteria and qualifications for the following post shall be substituted, namely:- “Chief General Manager (Administration and Human Resource)” [ADVT. IIl/4/Exty./122/ 13] NARENDRA KUMAR, Member (Administration) Foot note; The principal regulations were published vide notification No. NHAR-12011/1/95-Admn., dated llth ' March, 1996, in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part III, Section 4, dated the 11th March, 1996 and 4' last amended vide notification No. NHAI/11011/17/2012-HR.I (Vol. XV), dated 18th June, 2013, published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part III — Section 4, dated the let June, 2013. Explanatory Memorandum Anything done or omitted to be done prior to notification of this amendment shall not be affected by promulgation of this amendment. 1 -- 4 of 5 -- [atom—m4] ummmwmw 5 Name of post No. of post Classification . of post I 2 3 Chief General Manager 0] Group-A (Administration and Human ' Resource) Pay Band and Grade Pay Method of recruitment Age limit _ g (in resmact of transfer on deputation only) 4 5 6 i PB-4 (Rs.37,400—67,000) Selection through Search- 56 years with Grade Pay Rs.I0,000/— cum-Selection Committee Educational qualification and experience required Recruitment Criteria 7 8 G‘Essential Educational Qualification:- Degree of a recognized University. Desirable Educational Qualification:- Post Graduate Degree / Post Graduate Diploma in Management with specialization in Human Resources/ Personnel Management & Industrial Relations from a recognized University/ Institute; (a) or Degree in Law from a recognized University/ Institute. (b) Essential Experience:- Should be working in an analogous post or the post next below (General Manager/ Director or equivalent) for at least 6 years on a regular basis; and ' (ii) At least 18 years’ service in a Group—‘A’ post carrying the pay scale of Pay Band-3 (Rs.15600-39100) with Grade Pay Rs.5400/- or higher (in CDA Pattern) on a regular basis; In and (iii) Out of which at least 12 years experience in Administration/ Establishment/ Human Resource/ Personnel Management. Selection through Search-cum-Selection Committee:- (I) From amongst General Manager (Administration) of NHAI with six years’ regular service in that post; M possessing essential educational qualification and essential experience stipulated in Column 7. OR (2) By deputation from officers under the Central Government or State Governments or Union Territories or Universities or Recognized Research Institutions affiliated to Government of India or Public Sector Undertakings or Semi Government or Statutory or Autonomous Organizations and other Government Bodies:- (i) holding analogous posts on regular basis; or (ii) with three years of regular service in the posts in the pay scale of Pay Band-4 (Rs.37400-67000) with Grade Pay Rs,8900/- or equivalent; or (iii) with six years of regular service in the posts in the pay scale of Pay Band—4 (Rs. 37400-67000) with Grade Pay Rs. 8700/- or equivalent; and Possessing the essential educational qualification and essential experience stipulated in column 7. Period of deputation Period of appointment on selection shall be for an initial period of 3 years, extendable by two more years with the approval of the Government, subject to satisfactory performance. There shall be no bar to an officer getting a second term after going through the due process of selection. 3&9i_41/32 Printed by the Manager Government of India Press.
